channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Mayor Adams' Chief Advisor Ingrid Lewis-Martin steps down
item.description: Editor's Note: The video above originally aired on Sept. 25, 2024. NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams’ Chief Advisor Ingrid Lewis-Martin has stepped down, sources told PIX11 News. Lewis-Martin on Sunday announced she's leaving her post.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's tariffs threat is dividing business owners across the country
item.description: Businesses are divided over Trump's plan to impose sweeping tariffs. Some companies welcome the protection from foreign competition, while others worry about rising costs and retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: Here's what to know about the US push for stability in post-Assad Syria
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken has wrapped up perhaps his last Middle East visit as America’s top diplomat, with the aim of preventing Syria from spiraling out of control after the sudden ouster of President Bashar Assad. Blinken was one of several senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump seizes on drone controversy to mock Chris Christie
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked his ex-ally, former New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie, Saturday by seizing on the mysterious New Jersey drone controversy on his social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominees should ‘steer clear’ of undermining polio vaccine, McConnell says
item.description: Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell, a childhood polio survivor, said any of President-elect Donald Trump’s picks requiring Senate confirmation should “steer clear” of efforts to discredit the polio vaccine.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Donald Trump's presidential library to settle defamation lawsuit
item.description: ABC News has agreed to pay $15 million toward Donald Trump’s presidential library to settle a defamation lawsuit over anchor George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that the president-elect had been found civilly liable for raping writer E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pelosi has hip replacement surgery at a US military hospital in Germany after a fall
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has had hip replacement surgery at a U.S. military hospital in Germany after falling while at an event in Luxembourg with other members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken says American officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that led the overthrow of Bashar Assad’s government but is designated a foreign terrorist organization by the United States and others.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump brings political excitement to Army-Navy game
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance will attend the annual Army-Navy game in Landover, Maryland, on Saturday, and they are bringing some guests who are sure to stir debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mayor Adams seeks to deport migrants who commit crimes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams told President-elect Donald Trump’s border czar he would work with him to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City. Adams met with Tom Homan at Gracie Mansion to discuss Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Texas AG sues New York doctor for providing abortion pills across state lines
item.description: Both surgical and medication abortions are banned in Texas, and Friday’s lawsuit is the first legal test to see what happens when state abortion laws conflict with one another.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Unique among 'Person of the Year' designees, Donald Trump gets a fact-check from Time magazine
item.description: Donald Trump got something this year that no other person designated Time magazine's Person of the Year had ever received.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DC food workers pledge to make Trump officials unwelcome, echoing confrontations in first term
item.description: Washington, D.C., food workers vow to disrupt service for incoming Trump administration officials, echoing past harassment of his allies during the president-elect's first term in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Biden tells military children at Marine Corps Reserve toy drive to remember that they are loved
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Less than 10 minutes was all it took for a large pile of toys donated by the White House staff to disappear as Jill Biden and children from military families sorted them into boxes as part of the annual Marine Corps Reserve Toys for Tots campaign for the less fortunate.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny, acquitted in NYC subway chokehold, will join Trump's suite at football game
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Daniel Penny, a military veteran who choked an agitated New York subway rider and was acquitted of homicide this week, has been invited by Vice President-elect JD Vance to join Donald Trump's suite at the Army-Navy football game on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Raskin demands FBI vetting of Trump nominees, details on Epshteyn
item.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.) on Friday made a series of demands of the Trump transition team, asking for assurances President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees will undergo FBI vetting while also requesting details about aide Boris Epshteyn’s alleged profiting from promoting those seeking new posts.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas bill would allow Bitcoin to be used for taxes, donations
item.description: Texas is among 10 states considering legislation about the expanded use of cryptocurrency by its residents
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard struggles to woo senators ahead of confirmation fight
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard is struggling through her meetings with senators this week, sources told The Hill, highlighting the difficult path she faces in winning confirmation to be director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Border Report Live: 'Border czar' accepts Texas land as others push back on deportations
item.description: Tom Homan told Dr. Phil he's already working on a plan for borderlands the Texas offered the Trump administration's "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Lawmakers press for more briefings
item.description: "Law enforcement and other agencies continue to support New Jersey and investigate the reports," the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: About 3 in 10 are highly confident in Trump on Cabinet, spending or military oversight: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Americans may have recently elected Donald Trump to a second term, but that doesn't mean they have high confidence in his ability to choose well-qualified people for his Cabinet — or effectively manage government spending, the military and the White House, according to a new poll f
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota cities take different approaches to recreational cannabis market
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature set most of the rules about cultivation and sale of recreational marijuana in spring 2023. However, the state left several key decisions within local control, including whether cities can allow more than a set minimum of shops.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Amazon to contribute $1 million to Trump’s inauguration fund. Meta is also donating $1M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Amazon is planning to donate $1 million to President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration fund, a move that comes as major tech companies seek to improve their relationship with the incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House releases national strategy to combat Islamophobia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Thursday announced what it called the first-ever national strategy to counter Islamophobia, detailing more than 100 steps federal officials are taking to curb hate, violence, bias and discrimination against Muslims and Arab Americans.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: Asked at a Chinese Foreign Ministry briefing on Thursday about Trump's invitation, spokesperson Mao Ning responded: “I have nothing to share at present.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to U.S. and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: Ontario may considering barring American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Minnesota, Michigan and New York state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: New Yorkers could receive 'inflation refund' check from state: Here's how much
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Millions of New Yorkers would receive hundreds of dollars in an “inflation refund” payment from the state next year under a new proposal from Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
